deploy_url: pending — branch pushed, awaiting PR merge to main and Vercel auto-deploy
grounding_checkpoint_required: Navigate to /net-worth. Verify the '2022 Tesla (Vehicle)' row shows an 'Estimate Value' button alongside the existing 'Edit' button.
Click 'Estimate Value'. After 5–20 seconds loading state, confirm a median comparable price appears with listing count and 'Use This Value / Cancel' UI.
The estimated price should be a plausible 2022 Alberta Tesla Model Y market value (within ~20% of the $39,500 current balance).
Click 'Use This Value' — balance should update in the table. Refresh confirms DB write persisted.
Click 'Edit' to manually revert to the prior value if the estimate was incorrect.
If Vercel deployment fails due to @sparticuz/chromium bundle size exceeding 50 MB, the kill signal route returns 501 with bundle_limit_exceeded — button will show toast and open Edit mode.
unknowns: Pre-existing test failures: 154 tests failing across 25 test files — all pre-existed before this chunk (confirmed by checking that no new failures were introduced). These are not mine to fix per Principle 13.

AutoTrader.ca URL structure may change. The scraper uses two strategies (data-testid selectors + text pattern fallback) but AutoTrader.ca's actual HTML structure was not verifiable without a live browser. The grounding checkpoint will confirm scrape success or surface the need for selector tuning.

Vercel bundle size with @sparticuz/chromium: package.json already had @sparticuz/chromium@133.0.0 and puppeteer-core@24.43.1 from the WIP commit. If bundle size exceeds Vercel's 50 MB function limit, the kill signal path (501 + bundle_limit_exceeded) is in place and the UI gracefully falls back to toast + Edit mode.
